description: A December 1, 2013, referendum in Croatia asking if marriage should be constitutionally defined as a union between a man and a woman. The 'Yes' (against same-sex marriage) side won with 65.8% of the vote.

key_events.description: Catholic Church launches a nationwide campaign for the referendum.
Human rights groups organize protests against the proposed amendment.
Polling consistently shows the conservative side maintaining a steady lead.
Final results are confirmed with relatively low overall turnout.

calibration_notes: Exemplifies how cultural/moral issues can mobilize a specific segment of the electorate despite low overall interest.
